datasets.name: 2c8f935a
queryLines:
SELECT * 
FROM main.default.total_ap_job_run_state_analysis
WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max

datasets.name: 52556b40
queryLines:
SELECT
*,  
CASE
    WHEN :period = 'monthly' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-01') AS DATE)
    WHEN :period = 'daily' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS DATE)
END AS usage_date_new
FROM main.default.all_total_cost_and_quantity_workload
WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max

datasets.name: 7ef5daab
queryLines:
with temp as
  (SELECT
  region,
  workspace_id,
  workspace_name,
  sku_name,
  cluster_type,
  SUM(usage_quantity) AS total_usage_quantity,
  SUM(total_list_cost) AS total_list_cost
FROM main.default.all_total_cost_and_quantity_workload
WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
GROUP BY all)

SELECT 
  t.*
FROM 
  temp t
LIMIT CAST(:limit AS INT);

datasets.name: 079c901d
queryLines:
select *,
      CASE
        WHEN :period = 'monthly' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-01') AS DATE)
        WHEN :period = 'daily' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS DATE)
      END AS usage_date_new
from main.default.total_cost_and_quantity_interactive_cluster
WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max;

datasets.name: c5f70e15
queryLines:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT 
    region, workspace_id, workspace_name, workspace_url, cluster_id, cluster_name, sku_name, 
    driver_node_type, worker_node_type, worker_count, min_autoscale_workers, max_autoscale_workers, 
    auto_termination_minutes,
    --usage_date,
    SUM(total_cost) AS total_cost,                    -- sums already-rounded daily totals from the table
    SUM(total_usage_quantity_dbu) AS total_usage_quantity_dbu
  FROM main.default.all_purpose_cost_by_tags
  WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
  GROUP BY ALL
)

  SELECT b.*
FROM base b
  LIMIT CAST(:limit AS INT);

datasets.name: 028fc4c4
queryLines:
WITH usage_cost AS (
 select
    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    region,
    workspace_url,
    cluster_id,
    job_id,
    sku_name,
    sum(usage_quantity_dbu) as usage_quantity_dbu,
    sum(total_cost) as total_cost
     from main.default.ap_cluster_by_job_runs_usage_cost
where usage_date between :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
 group by all
),

total_job_runs AS (
   SELECT
    cluster_id,
    cluster_name,
    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    max(job_run_date) as job_run_date,
    sum(total_job_runs) as total_job_runs,
    sum(successful_runs) as successful_runs,
    sum(failed_runs) as failed_runs,
    avg(failure_rate_percent) as failure_rate_percent,
    avg(success_rate_percent) as success_rate_percent
  FROM
    main.default.ap_cluster_by_job_runs_total_job_runs
  WHERE
    job_run_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
    group by all
),

temp AS (
  SELECT 
    usage.region,
    j.workspace_id,
    j.workspace_name,
    CASE 
      WHEN usage.sku_name LIKE '%SERVERLESS%' THEN 'ALL_PURPOSE_SERVERLESS'
      WHEN j.cluster_id IS NULL THEN 'UNKNOWN'
      ELSE j.cluster_id
    END AS cluster_id,
    CASE 
      WHEN usage.sku_name LIKE '%SERVERLESS%' THEN 'ALL_PURPOSE_SERVERLESS'
      WHEN j.cluster_name IS NULL THEN 'UNKNOWN'
      ELSE j.cluster_name
    END AS cluster_name,
    usage.sku_name,
    j.total_job_runs,
    usage.total_cost,
    usage.total_cost * 0.454 AS potential_saving,
    j.job_run_date AS last_used_date
  FROM usage_cost usage
  INNER JOIN total_job_runs j
    ON usage.workspace_id = j.workspace_id AND usage.cluster_id = j.cluster_id
)


  SELECT 
    t.*
  FROM temp t
  LIMIT CAST(:limit AS INT);

datasets.name: 630ea6a2
queryLines:
with base as
(
  select *,
  CASE
    WHEN :period = 'monthly' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-01') AS DATE)
    WHEN :period = 'daily' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS DATE)
  END AS usage_date_new
  from main.default.total_cost_and_quantity_job
  where usage_date between :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
)



SELECT 
  t.*
FROM 
  base t
LIMIT CAST(:limit AS INT);

datasets.name: a7d92282
queryLines:
with base as
(
select 
workspace_id, workspace_name, region, 
sku_name, job_id_or_pipeline_name, job_or_pipeline_name, run_type, run_result, avg_cpu_utilization, 
peak_cpu_utilization, avg_cpu_disk_wait, max_cpu_disk_wait, avg_memory_utilization, max_memory_utilization, 
sum(max_potential_savings) as max_potential_savings, sum(total_job_runs) as total_job_runs, sum(success_count) as success_count, 
sum(failure_count) as failure_count, AVG(success_percent) as success_percent, avg(not_successful_percent) as not_successful_percent, 
sum(total_success_cost) as total_success_cost, sum(total_failure_cost) as total_failure_cost,sum(total_usage_quantity_dbu) as total_usage_quantity_dbu,
sum(total_cost) as total_cost
from main.default.total_cost_and_quantity_job
where usage_date between :date_range.min and :date_range.max
group by all
)


SELECT 
  t.*
FROM 
  base t
LIMIT CAST(:limit AS INT);

datasets.name: d2f17553
queryLines:
with base as
(select region, workspace_id, workspace_name, job_id, job_or_pipeline_name, sku_name, 
cluster_id, cluster_name, driver_node_type, worker_node_type, worker_count, min_autoscale_workers, 
max_autoscale_workers, total_usage_quantity_dbu, total_list_cost
from main.default.jobs_no_scaling 
where usage_date between :date_range.min and :date_range.max)

  SELECT 
    t.*
  FROM base t
  LIMIT CAST(:limit AS INT);

datasets.name: ad4f3bfc
55fd9c7a
queryLines:
SELECT *,      
    CASE
        WHEN :period = 'monthly' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-01') AS DATE)
        WHEN :period = 'daily' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS DATE)
    END AS usage_date_new
FROM main.default.serverless_usage_metrics
WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
ORDER BY total_cost DESC;


-- select * from system.billing.usage where product_features.is_serverless limit 10

datasets.name: ce593ae1
queryLines:
SELECT
    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    run_as,
    sku_name,
    SUM(total_cost) AS total_cost,
    SUM(total_dbu) AS total_dbu
FROM main.default.serverless_usage_metrics
WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
AND run_as is not Null
GROUP BY 
    run_as, 
    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    run_as,
    sku_name
ORDER BY total_cost DESC;

datasets.name: aecfbd27
queryLines:

    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    run_as,
    sku_name,
    resource_id,
    resource_nm,
    SUM(total_cost) AS total_cost,
    SUM(total_dbu) AS total_dbu
FROM main.default.serverless_usage_metrics
WHERE billing_origin_product = 'JOBS'
  AND usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
GROUP BY 
    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    run_as,
    sku_name,
    resource_id,
    resource_nm
ORDER BY total_dbu DESC;

datasets.name: 93a0cafc
queryLines:
SELECT 
    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    run_as,
    sku_name,
    resource_id,
    resource_nm,
    SUM(total_cost) AS total_cost,
    SUM(total_dbu) AS total_dbu
FROM main.default.serverless_usage_metrics
WHERE billing_origin_product = 'INTERACTIVE'
  AND usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
GROUP BY 
    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    run_as,
    sku_name,
    resource_id,
    resource_nm
ORDER BY total_dbu DESC;

    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    run_as,
    sku_name,
    resource_id,
    resource_nm,

    SUM(total_dbu) AS total_dbu

GROUP BY

ORDER BY total_dbu DESC;

datasets.name: ea1affbc
queryLines:
select *,
      CASE
        WHEN :period = 'monthly' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-01') AS DATE)
        WHEN :period = 'daily' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS DATE)
      END AS usage_date_new
from main.default.all_total_cost_and_quantity_warehouse
WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max

SELECT *
FROM main.default.warehouse_most_uptime where start_time BETWEEN :date_range.min and :date_range.max

datasets.name: a09a2eae
queryLines:
select 
    workspace_id,
    workspace_name,
    warehouse_id,
    warehouse_name,
    warehouse_status,
    last(warehouse_size) as warehouse_size ,
    min_clusters,
    max_clusters,
    MAX(event_time) as last_activity,
    DATEDIFF(CURRENT_DATE(), DATE(MAX(event_time))) as days_idle 
    from main.default.idle_warehouse
    where event_time between :date_range.min and :date_range.max
    group by all
    HAVING DATEDIFF(CURRENT_DATE(), DATE(MAX(event_time))) >= 30
    ORDER BY days_idle DESC

datasets.name: b9f5af51
queryLines: select * from main.default.warehouse_channel_preview  where change_time BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max group by all

datasets.name: 726b558c
queryLines:
select *,
      CASE
        WHEN :period = 'monthly' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-01') AS DATE)
        WHEN :period = 'daily' THEN CAST(DATE_FORMAT(usage_date, 'yyyy-MM-dd') AS DATE)
      END AS usage_date_new
from main.default.all_total_cost_and_quantity_warehouse
WHERE usage_date BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max

datasets.name: 2075ddd2
queryLines:
SELECT
    *
FROM
   main.default.auto_stop_minutes_warehouse
where
change_time BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max
order by auto_stop_minutes DESC

datasets.name: 8725e3f6
queryLines:
SELECT
    *
FROM
   main.default.objects_without_tags_best_practices
where
time BETWEEN :date_range.min AND :date_range.max

frame.description: Displays the total discounted cost across all compute workloads for the selected filters.
frame.title: Cost for All Workloads ($)

frame.description: Displays the total discounted cost across all compute Interactive Clusters for the selected filters
frame.title: Cost Breakdown by Cluster type

lines:
### 🧭 Summary

 ---

This dashboard provides a comprehensive, high-level view of `cost`,`utilization`, `Potential savings` and `efficiency`. It covers compute costs and resource utilization across environments and workspaces, helping users quickly assess performance, monitor cost efficiency, and identify optimization opportunities

frame.title: Potential Savings Opportunity for All-Purpose Clusters
frame.description: Note: This savings can be achieved by running the job runs on Job clusters instead of all purpose.

frame.title: Potential Savings Opportunity for Job Clusters(Last 90 days only)
frame.description: Note: Highlighted savings can be achieved through optimal CPU utilization.
Displays the total discounted cost across all compute workloads for the selected filters.
frame.title: YTD Cost for All Workloads ($)
Aggregated Cost Trend
frame.description:
SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) in Databricks refers to the type of compute workload being used. This query summarizes daily DBU usage and list price cost across workspaces, grouped by high-level SKU categories such as ALL_PURPOSE, JOBS, DLT, SQL, MODEL_SERVING / INFERENCE, and CONNECTIVITY. It filters data by region and date range, and maps resource groups to environments. Hovering over the chart reveals DBU usage, cost, SKU, and date for each data point based on the selected filters.
Note: Data is only available for the past 365 days due to Databricks' retention policy, even if a longer date range is selected.

frame.title: Cost breakdown and Potential Savings for All-Purpose clusters
frame.description:
Note: 
1. Below highlighted savings can be achieved by running the job runs on Job clusters instead of all purpose.
2. Below highlighted total cost and savings is available for last 90 days.

frame.title: Cost Breakdown and Potential Savings for Job clusters
frame.description: Note: Highlighted savings can be achieved through optimal CPU utilization and is available for last 90 days.

frame.title: Potential Savings Opportunity Trend for All Purpose and Job clusters
frame.description: Note: Savings for Job clusters available only for last 90 days

frame.description: Allowed values - "daily/monthly"

Note: Data is only available for the past 365 days due to Databricks' retention policy, even if a longer date range is selected.

Total job runs on All-Purpose clusters

Displays the total number of job runs executed for the selected filters
Note: Data is only available for the past 365 days due to Databricks' retention policy, even if a longer date range is selected.
